questions.prompt: Define a tool for LangGraph agent?

choices.text: @tool decorator on function with descriptive docstring. `@tool def search(query: str) -> str: """Search the web.""" return ...`. Docstring becomes LLM-visible description; LLM uses to decide when to call. Use Pydantic args_schema for complex inputs. ToolNode auto-executes called tools. Built-in tools available (TavilySearch, PythonREPL, etc.). Handle errors gracefully — return error text vs. raising.

questions.explanation: @tool decorator + descriptive docstring. ToolNode executes. Built-in tools available.

questions.prompt: Agent has 25 tools; performance poor + confused. Fix?

choices.text: Reduce tool count. LLMs decide poorly when too many tools (selection paralysis); typically 5-10 tools max per agent. Options: 1) Combine related tools (single 'search' vs. 'web_search' + 'doc_search' + 'image_search' — single multi-purpose). 2) Hierarchical agents — supervisor decides which sub-agent handles; each sub-agent has 5 tools. 3) Tool routing — classifier picks relevant tools; agent gets subset. 4) Eliminate unused tools — what's actually called? Drop the rest. Pro habit: minimum viable toolset; add only what's needed. The 'kitchen sink' tool list assumption hurts quality. Less is more for agent decision-making.

questions.explanation: Reduce to 5-10 tools max. Combine/eliminate. Hierarchical agents for more capability.
